attractions.name: Broyhill Fountain
attractions.description: The Broyhill Fountain is a prominent landmark and memorial located on the University of Nebraska–Lincoln City Campus. Dedicated to the memory of student Lynn Diann Broyhill, the current contemporary design features ten water jets and massive granite boulders, serving as a central gathering place for students and visitors. (Nebraska Today, UNL History)
attractions.region: University of Nebraska–Lincoln City Campus (Downtown Lincoln)
attractions.hours: Jets run daily from 7:30 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. during the operating season. (Nebraska Today)
attractions.admission: Free (Nebraska Today)
highlights: Memorial dedicated to Lynn Diann Broyhill, a UNL student who passed away in 1966. (Nebraska Today)
Contemporary design featuring 10 water jets and large granite chunks from South Dakota. (UNL History)
Holds approximately 30,000 gallons of water and utilizes a 24-hour filtration system. (Nebraska Today)
Equipped with an anemometer that automatically shuts down jets during high winds. (Nebraska Today)
Features energy-efficient LED lighting that can display a variety of colors for special events. (Nebraska Today)
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to dip their feet and legs into the water, but wading, climbing on rocks, and swimming are prohibited for safety and maintenance reasons. (Nebraska Today)
attractions.nearby: Nebraska Union, Love Library, Sheldon Museum of Art, Morrill Hall (University of Nebraska State Museum) (UNL Maps)

attractions.name: Crane River Brewpub & Café (Everand)
attractions.description: Crane River Brewpub & Café was Lincoln's second brewpub, opening in October 1992 and becoming a cornerstone of the downtown craft beer scene. It was famous for its signature 26.5-foot-long quilt, "Crane River Morning," and its diverse selection of handcrafted beers before closing in May 2003 (Everand).
attractions.hours: Mon-Sat: 11:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sun: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Historical estimate based on GitHub)
attractions.admission: Free (Everand)
highlights: Lincoln's second brewpub, established in 1992 (Everand)
Home to the signature 26.5-foot "Crane River Morning" quilt by Shelly Burge (The Quilt Show)
Featured unique local brews like Carhenge Wheat and Flaming Crane Chili Beer (Howder Family)
A pioneer in the downtown Lincoln dining and craft beer movement (Everand)
The iconic quilt is now preserved at the International Quilt Museum (The Quilt Show)
attractions.tips: Though the brewpub is permanently closed, visitors can still view its famous 'Crane River Morning' quilt at the International Quilt Museum on the UNL East Campus (The Quilt Show).
attractions.nearby: Historic Haymarket District, University of Nebraska-Lincoln, Lied Center for Performing Arts (Lincoln Convention & Visitors Bureau)

attractions.name: Dish Restaurant
attractions.description: DISH is a Modern American restaurant located in the heart of downtown Lincoln, specializing in seasonal, ingredient-driven cuisine. Led by James Beard Award semifinalist Rachel McGill, the culinary team focuses on local and sustainable products to create an ever-changing, innovative menu. (Dish Restaurant Official Site)
attractions.address: 1100 O Street, Lincoln, NE 68508 (Dish Restaurant Official Site)
attractions.hours: Tuesday - Thursday: 5:00 pm to 8:30 pm; Friday - Saturday: 5:00 pm to 9:30 pm (Dish Restaurant Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free (Menu items range from approximately $6 to $42) (Dish Restaurant Menu)
highlights: James Beard Foundation's Best Chef: Midwest Award semifinalist Rachel McGill (Dish Restaurant Official Site)
Seasonally driven, locally sourced rotating menu (Dish Restaurant Official Site)
Upscale dining experience with handcrafted cocktails (Dish Restaurant Official Site)
Located in a historic downtown corner at 11th and O Streets (Dish Restaurant Official Site)
Outdoor patio dining available upon request (weather permitting) (Dish Restaurant Official Site)
attractions.tips: Reservations are strongly encouraged for parties of all sizes and may be required on busy evenings. Note that the restaurant does not accommodate takeout at this time to prioritize the dine-in experience. (Dish Restaurant Official Site)
attractions.nearby: Marcus Lincoln Grand Cinema, Kiechel Fine Art, Center for Great Plains Studies, The Grand Manse, and the University of Nebraska-Lincoln (Tripadvisor, Downtown Lincoln Association)

attractions.name: Duffy's Tavern (Duffy's Tavern)
attractions.description: Duffy's Tavern is a historic downtown Lincoln institution that has been serving patrons since 1936. Known famously as the "Home of the Fishbowl," the venue is a cornerstone of the local music scene, having hosted legendary acts such as Nirvana and The Flaming Lips. (Duffy's Tavern, Curious Places with Kurt)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 4:00 PM - 2:00 AM; Opens at 8:00 AM on Husker Home Gamedays (Duffy's Tavern)
attractions.admission: Free entry for the bar; ticket prices for live music and special events vary (Duffy's Tavern, Vivid Seats)
highlights: Famous "Fishbowl" cocktails with over 50 drink options ([Duffy\
, "Historic live music stage that has hosted Nirvana, 311, and Bright Eyes ([Duffy
, 'Large outdoor beer garden featuring a fire pit and big screens (Tripadvisor)',
s Tavern])",
attractions.tips: Don't miss the famous Fishbowl cocktail, but be prepared to share it with friends using their iconic giant straws. Check the back patio for a more relaxed vibe on warm nights, and always check the online calendar for live music and comedy show schedules. (Curious Places with Kurt, Yelp)
attractions.nearby: Tugboat Gallery, The Bourbon Theatre, Lincoln Children's Museum, Nebraska History Museum, Nebraska State Capitol (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Great Plains Art Museum (incorporating the Christlieb Gallery) (Official Website)
attractions.description: The Great Plains Art Museum is a fine arts institution dedicated to collecting, preserving, and interpreting art and literature that reflects the diverse cultures and natural environments of the Great Plains (Official Website). It was founded in 1981 following a significant donation of Western art and Americana from Dr. John and Elizabeth Christlieb, which remains the cornerstone of its permanent collection (Wikipedia).
attractions.region: Downtown Lincoln, University of Nebraska-Lincoln City Campus (Downtown Lincoln Association).
attractions.hours: Tuesday–Saturday: 10 a.m.–5 p.m. (Official Website). Closed Sundays, Mondays, major U.S. holidays, university breaks, and home football game Saturdays.
attractions.admission: Free admission for all visitors (Official Website).
highlights: The Christlieb Collection, featuring bronze sculptures and paintings by renowned Western artists like Frederic Remington and Charles Russell (Wikipedia).
Regularly rotating exhibitions in multiple galleries, including the Main, South, West, and Mezzanine galleries (Official Website).
A library of Western Americana with rare books and literature focused on the history and culture of the Great Plains (Official Website).
Contemporary works by regional artists, including photographs by Michael Forsberg and paintings by John Philip Falter (Wikipedia).
Indigenous ceramics and permanent collection spotlights that showcase diverse cultural artistry (Official Website).
attractions.tips: Off-street parking is available at the Que Place Parking Garage adjacent to the museum (Official Website). Visitors can request guided tours in advance or explore the galleries via self-guided visits (Official Website).
attractions.nearby: Lied Center for Performing Arts, Sheldon Museum of Art, Lincoln Children's Museum, and Nebraska History Museum (Downtown Lincoln Association).

attractions.name: HopCat Lincoln
attractions.description: HopCat Lincoln is a vibrant craft beer bar and restaurant located in the West Haymarket district, celebrated for its extensive selection of over 100 local and regional beers on tap. The casual, sports-friendly atmosphere features 52-inch TVs for game days and a diverse menu of scratch-made comfort food, including their famous award-winning Cosmik Fries. HopCat, Tripadvisor

attractions.region: West Haymarket / Downtown Lincoln HopCat, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Kimball Recital Hall Lied Center
attractions.description: Kimball Recital Hall serves as the primary performance venue for the Glenn Korff School of Music, hosting concerts by faculty, students, and guest artists (Lied Center). This acoustically renowned hall features an elliptical roof and round ceiling designed to provide an exceptional physical setting for musical recitals (Lied Center).
attractions.address: 1113 R St, Lincoln, NE 68508 Visit Lincoln
attractions.region: Downtown Lincoln / UNL City Campus Visit Lincoln
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: Monday-Friday, 11:00 AM - 3:00 PM; Doors typically open 60-90 minutes prior to scheduled performances (Lied Center, Vivid Seats)
attractions.days_open: Monday-Friday (Ticket Office); performance days vary by event schedule (Lied Center, UNL Newsroom)
attractions.admission: Many student and faculty recitals are Free and open to the public (UNL Newsroom); professional performances range from approximately $10 to $42 (Lincoln Friends of Chamber Music)
highlights: Home to the University of Nebraska-Lincoln's Glenn Korff School of Music Lied Center
Recently renovated in 2025 with an expanded lobby and updated seating UNL Newsroom
Acoustically astounding design with an elliptical roof and round ceiling Lied Center
Seating capacity of 850 audience members across the main floor and balcony Visit Lincoln
Equipped with an orchestra pit capable of accommodating a 40-member ensemble Lied Center
Hosts a wide range of performances from solo recitals to large orchestral concerts UNL Newsroom
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30-60 minutes early to secure parking in nearby downtown garages like the University Square Garage (TicketSmarter, Lied Center). Be aware of venue bag policies, which generally favor small clear bags or hand-held clutches (Vivid Seats). Many University-led performances are also live-webcast for remote viewing (UNL Newsroom).
attractions.nearby: Lied Center for Performing Arts (Wikipedia), Sheldon Museum of Art (Lied Center), Downtown Lincoln shopping and dining (Downtown Lincoln Association)

attractions.name: Lied Center for Performing Arts
attractions.description: The Lied Center for Performing Arts is Nebraska's premier venue for world-class Broadway productions, music, dance, and theater. Located on the University of Nebraska-Lincoln campus, it serves as a cultural hub, hosting both international icons and regional artists to educate, inspire, and entertain the community. Lied Center About, Visit Nebraska
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: Monday - Friday, 11:00 AM - 3:00 PM; Performance Days: Two hours prior to start time until 15 minutes into the show. Administrative Office: Monday - Friday, 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Closed 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM for lunch). Lied Center Directions & Parking, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Box Office); Performance days vary including weekends.
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by performance; recent listings range from $24 to over $400 for major Broadway shows. Free tickets are occasionally provided to children and students through the Performance Fund. SeatGeek, Lied Center Performance Fund
highlights: Host to major Broadway tours like Mamma Mia!, Shucked, and Beetlejuice.
Home to the Lincoln Symphony Orchestra and diverse musical performances.
Features the Johnny Carson Theater for more intimate productions.
Offers extensive education and outreach programs for PK-12 and university students.
State-of-the-art facility with a seating capacity of over 2,000.
Regularly presents world-renowned artists such as Wynton Marsalis and world-class dance companies.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to enjoy pre-performance talks often held before shows. Valet parking is available and can be reserved in advance through the Lied Center app. Downtown parking garages like Que Place and Larson Building are conveniently located nearby. Dress varies by event, but most patrons wear business casual or festive attire. Lied Center FAQ
attractions.nearby: Great Plains Art Museum, Sheldon Museum of Art, Lincoln Children's Museum, University of Nebraska State Museum, Kimball Recital Hall. Downtown Lincoln Association, Sheldon Museum of Art

attractions.name: Lincoln Children's Museum
attractions.description: The Lincoln Children's Museum invites families to create, discover, and learn through the power of play. Spanning three floors and 23,300 square feet, it offers hands-on exhibits and imaginative programs designed for children and their grown-ups to explore together. Information sourced from Tripadvisor and official website.
attractions.hours: Mon: 9am – 5pm; Tue: Closed; Wed: 8am – 5pm; Thu – Sat: 9am – 5pm; Sun: 11am – 5pm. Sensory-friendly hours: Wed & Thu 3pm – 5pm. Schedule from official website.
attractions.days_open: Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Closed Tuesdays)
attractions.admission: Adults (16+): $12.95, Seniors (65+): $11.95, Youth (19 months-15 years): $14.95, Infants (0-18 months): Free, Members: Free. Note: Admission is 50% off after 4pm for all ages. Prices cited from Whichmuseum and official website.
highlights: Three-story Cuckoo Clock with Kazoo the bird
LNKLab STEM discovery area for older kids
Union Bank & Trust real-world money exhibit
Tiny House by Woods Bros Realty
Water Works interactive play area
Zoetis Vet Clinic and Bryan Health role-play areas
attractions.tips: Children must be accompanied by an adult at all times. The main entrance is on the south side of the building facing the Nebraska History Museum. Visit after 4:00 PM to take advantage of 50% off admission. Tips from official website.

attractions.name: Memorial Stadium (Tom Osborne Field at Memorial Stadium)
attractions.description: Memorial Stadium, known as 'The Sea of Red,' is the historic home venue for the Nebraska Cornhuskers football team on the University of Nebraska–Lincoln campus. Dedicated in 1923 to honor Nebraskans who served in several wars, it holds an NCAA-record sellout streak dating back to 1962. The stadium serves as a iconic symbol of collegiate athletics and community pride in Nebraska. (Wikipedia))
attractions.region: University of Nebraska-Lincoln Campus, Downtown Lincoln
attractions.hours: Public Access (Self-Guided/Hall of Champions): Wednesday–Sunday 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM; Guided Tours (By Appointment): Wednesday–Friday 10:00 AM – 3:30 PM (Feb–July) and 12:00 PM – 3:30 PM (Aug–Jan). (University of Nebraska)
attractions.days_open: Wednesday–Sunday (General Public Access); No public access Monday–Tuesday or during home football game weeks. (University of Nebraska)
attractions.admission: Guided Tours: Free (requires registration at least 2 weeks in advance); Self-guided areas: Free. Gameday tickets vary by event. (University of Nebraska)
highlights: NCAA-record sellout streak dating back to 1962. (Wikipedia))
Tom Osborne Field, dedicated to the legendary Hall of Fame coach. (Wikipedia))
Iconic corner inscriptions honoring Nebraskan veterans. (University of Nebraska)
The 'Tunnel Walk' tradition where players take the field to 'Sirius'. (Wikipedia))
Osborne Legacy Complex Hall of Champions featuring Nebraska’s five national championship trophies. (University of Nebraska)
Record-breaking host for 'Volleyball Day in Nebraska' with over 92,000 fans. (Wikipedia))
attractions.tips: Guided tours must be requested at least two weeks in advance through the official athletics website. Visitors should park in public pay-to-park garages or use meters, as on-site parking is restricted. Visit during non-gameday weeks for the best access to the Hall of Champions and field. (University of Nebraska)
attractions.nearby: Pinnacle Bank Arena, University of Nebraska State Museum (Morrill Hall), Sheldon Museum of Art, Haymarket District. (Downtown Lincoln Association)

attractions.name: Nebraska Governor's Mansion (also known as Nebraska Governor's Residence)
attractions.description: The Nebraska Governor's Mansion is a modified Georgian Colonial home that has served as the official residence of Nebraska's governor and family since March 17, 1958. This grand 15,340-square-foot brick building sits on a landscaped city block and features 27 major rooms, including a formal dining room and a unique doll collection of the state's first ladies. Office of Governor Jim Pillen, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Thursday: 1:00 PM – 3:15 PM (by appointment only; closed mid-December to early March for public tours) Office of Governor Jim Pillen
attractions.days_open: Thursday (Public tours by appointment only) Office of Governor Jim Pillen
attractions.admission: Free (No charge for admission) Office of Governor Jim Pillen, VisitNebraska.com
highlights: Unique collection of dolls representing Nebraska's first ladies in their inaugural gowns
Elegant 15,340-square-foot modified Georgian Colonial architecture with 27 major rooms
Beautifully landscaped grounds featuring gardens, walking paths, and a fountain
Formal dining room and public function spaces on the first floor
Historical doorknob preserved from the original governor's mansion
Annual 'Hometown Christmas' event and holiday decorations during December
attractions.tips: Tours must be scheduled at least two weeks in advance, and all guest names must be provided beforehand. Arrive at the north Pedestrian Gate and ring the buzzer; note that no backpacks, bags, or strollers are allowed inside. Office of Governor Jim Pillen
attractions.nearby: Nebraska State Capitol, Thomas P. Kennard House (Nebraska Statehood Memorial), Ferguson House, Hazel Abel Park, Lincoln Children's Museum, Sunken Gardens Tripadvisor, VisitNebraska.com

attractions.name: Nebraska History Museum (History Nebraska)
attractions.description: The Nebraska History Museum showcases 12,000 years of the state's rich history through diverse artifacts and stories. It features exhibits on indigenous cultures, early statehood, and the cultural evolution of many people who have made Nebraska their home (History Nebraska).
attractions.address: 131 Centennial Mall N, Lincoln, NE 68508 (History Nebraska)
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Saturday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Sunday – Monday: Closed (History Nebraska)
attractions.admission: Adults (19+): $5; Children (5-18): $3; Under 5: Free; Students, Members, and School Groups: Free (History Nebraska)
highlights: Piecing Together the Past: Exploring 13,000 years of history with archeologists (History Nebraska)
Nebraska Unwrapped: Selections from the extensive museum collections (Visit Nebraska)
Play Unplugged: An exhibit exploring the history of play (Visit Nebraska)
Portraits of Survival: A photography display sharing impactful human stories (History Nebraska)
Extensive collections with over 125,000 historical objects (History Nebraska)
Made in Nebraska exhibit featuring locally produced historical items (History Nebraska)
attractions.tips: The museum is donation-based for some but typically has a modest $5 fee; it's a relatively small museum that can be toured in 1-2 hours (Tripadvisor). It is part of the Museums for All program, offering reduced admission for SNAP EBT cardholders (History Nebraska).
attractions.nearby: Lincoln Children's Museum, University of Nebraska State Museum (Morrill Hall), Sheldon Museum of Art, Memorial Stadium, and the Nebraska State Capitol (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Nebraska State Capitol
attractions.description: The Nebraska State Capitol, constructed from 1922 to 1932, is a National Historic Landmark known for its unique 'Tower on the Plains' design. Standing 400 feet tall, it features a majestic domed tower and a low spreading base adorned with intricate mosaics, sculptures, and murals representing Nebraska's natural and social history. It is the seat of Nebraska's government and home to the nation's only unicameral state legislature. (Wikipedia, VisitNebraska.com)
attractions.region: Downtown Lincoln (Capitol Square) (Nebraska Legislature, Nebraska State Capitol)
attractions.hours: Mon-Fri: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sat & Holidays: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sun: 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM (Nebraska State Capitol)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Except Thanksgiving Day, the Friday after, Christmas Day, and New Year’s Day) (Nebraska State Capitol)
attractions.admission: Free (VisitNebraska.com, Nebraska State Capitol)
highlights: 400-foot tower topped by 'The Sower' bronze statue (Wikipedia)
Ornate interior featuring extensive mosaic ceilings and floors (Tripadvisor)
14th-floor observation deck with panoramic views of Lincoln (Nebraska State Capitol)
Home to the only Unicameral (single-house) legislature in the U.S. (Wikipedia)
First truly vernacular state capitol design by architect Bertram Grosvenor Goodhue (VisitNebraska.com)
Nebraska Hall of Fame featuring busts of famous residents (Tripadvisor)
attractions.tips: Take the free guided tour offered on the hour to fully appreciate the building's history and art. Visit the 14th-floor observation deck for the best views of the city. Parking is available on surrounding streets, but the north entrance may be closed in winter temperatures. (Nebraska State Capitol, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Governor's Mansion, Saint Mary Catholic Church, Sunken Gardens, Lincoln Children's Museum, University of Nebraska State Museum, Memorial Stadium (Tripadvisor)

attractions.description: The Nebraska Union is a central hub for student life at the University of Nebraska–Lincoln, providing a sense of home and community for students, faculty, and visitors. It features a wide variety of services including dining options, meeting spaces, study lounges, and student organization offices, welcoming over 2 million visitors annually. Nebraska Union About

attractions.name: Nebraska's Centennial Mall (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Nebraska's Centennial Mall is a seven-block public space stretching from the Nebraska State Capitol to the University of Nebraska-Lincoln campus. It serves as a celebration of the state's past, present, and future, featuring commemorative plazas, public art, and green spaces designed to be the state's \"front door.\" (Wikipedia, City of Lincoln)
attractions.address: 1501 K Street, Lincoln, NE 68508 (City of Lincoln)
attractions.hours: Sunday-Friday: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Saturday: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM (Note: Official site lists Saturday as 5 PM-11 PM, but typical park hours are consistent across the week) (City of Lincoln)
attractions.admission: Free (Lincoln Parks Foundation)
highlights: Chief Standing Bear Statue (Lincoln Parks Foundation)
Spirit of Nebraska Pathway with commemorative tiles (City of Lincoln)
Decorative water features and flower gardens (City of Lincoln)
Dr. Susan La Flesche Picotte Sculpture (City of Lincoln)
Themed plazas including \\"We the People\\" and \\"Mosaic of Nebraskans\\" (City of Lincoln)
Scenic views connecting the State Capitol and UNL (Lincoln Parks Foundation)
attractions.tips: Use the QR codes on the Spirit of Nebraska Pathway tiles to learn more about the state's history. Brochures for walking tours are available at the State Capitol and the Nebraska History Museum. The mall is a popular spot for photography, especially with the Capitol in the background. (Lincoln Parks Foundation, Lincoln.org)
attractions.nearby: Nebraska State Capitol, Nebraska History Museum, University of Nebraska-Lincoln, Sheldon Museum of Art, Lincoln Children's Museum (Lincoln Parks Foundation, Lincoln.org)

attractions.name: O Street Corridor
attractions.description: O Street serves as the primary thoroughfare and "spine" of Lincoln, Nebraska, dividing the city north and south. The downtown portion is the city's largest bar and nightclub district, featuring a dense concentration of restaurants, live music venues, and retail shops. It is currently the focus of "Project O Street," a $35 million revitalization effort to enhance infrastructure, safety, and public amenities. (Nebraska Life Magazine, City of Lincoln)
attractions.address: O Street (9th to 16th Streets), Lincoln, NE 68508
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours; Tuesday: Open 24 hours; Wednesday: Open 24 hours; Thursday: Open 24 hours; Friday: Open 24 hours; Saturday: Open 24 hours; Sunday: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours vary) (Downtown Lincoln)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Lincoln)
highlights: Historically part of the Omaha-Lincoln-Denver (O-L-D) Highway, established in 1911. (Nebraska Life Magazine)
Home to the 14th Street Music District, featuring iconic venues like the Bourbon Theatre and Zoo Bar. (City of Lincoln)
Often cited as one of the longest straight main streets in the world, extending over 20 miles within city limits. (Nebraska Life Magazine)
Center of Lincoln's nightlife with the city's highest density of bars and nightclubs. (Wikipedia)
Ongoing "Project O Street" reinvestment includes new water mains, streetscaping, and pedestrian improvements. (City of Lincoln)
attractions.tips: Expect construction-related detours and sidewalk closures through 2027 due to the "Project O Street" revitalization. Utilize the "Park & Go" garages (like Rampark at 12th & P) for the most reliable parking options. Visit during "First Friday" events to experience the local art scene and gallery openings. (City of Lincoln, Downtown Lincoln)
attractions.nearby: Nebraska State Capitol, University of Nebraska-Lincoln, Historic Haymarket District, Sunken Gardens, Sheldon Museum of Art (Visit Nebraska)

attractions.name: The Green Gateau
attractions.description: The Green Gateau is an upper-scale neighborhood restaurant in the heart of downtown Lincoln, inspired by European country inns. It features French-influenced contemporary American cuisine, offering a cozy and inviting atmosphere with a seasonally rotating menu and one of Nebraska's most comprehensive wine selections.

attractions.name: The Railyard
attractions.description: The Railyard is Lincoln, Nebraska's premier outdoor entertainment district located in the heart of the West Haymarket. This vibrant venue features over 13 restaurants and bars, live entertainment, and "The Cube," a massive digital screen for sports and community events. Lincoln.org, Downtown Lincoln Association

Completed in 2013 for $179 million, it serves as the home for the University of Nebraska's men's and women's basketball teams and acts as the city's primary large-scale entertainment venue. The 470,400-square-foot facility features three public concourses, 36 executive suites, and 73 concession points of sale (Pinnacle Bank Arena, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Antelope Park
attractions.description: Antelope Park is a sprawling 92.93-acre regional park in Lincoln, Nebraska, featuring an extensive array of amenities and unique attractions. The park is home to the renowned Sunken Gardens, the Veterans Memorial Garden, and the city's largest playground area, making it a central hub for community recreation. It is unofficially divided into North, Triangle, and South sections, with the Billy Wolff and Rock Island trails providing scenic routes for walking and biking.

attractions.admission: Free; however, attractions within the park like the Lincoln Children's Zoo (Adults $15.95, Seniors/Children $13.95) and Jim Ager Golf Course (Adults $10.00) have separate fees.
highlights: Sunken Gardens featuring thousands of annuals and themed displays
Veterans Memorial Garden honoring those who served in the U.S. armed forces
Hamann Rose Garden and Strolling Garden
Lincoln's largest and most expansive playground area for children
Jim Ager Memorial Junior Golf Course designed for young golfers
Integration with the Billy Wolff and Rock Island biking and walking trails
attractions.tips: Visit the Sunken Gardens in the spring to see thousands of tulips or in the summer for peak annual blooms. The park is very large, so utilize the Billy Wolff and Rock Island trails to travel between different sections like the North and South areas. If visiting the Lincoln Children's Zoo, consider buying animal feed in advance for a more interactive experience.

attractions.name: Arbor Day Farm
attractions.description: Arbor Day Farm is a 260-acre national historic treasure and the birthplace of Arbor Day, dedicated to the mission of inspiring people to plant, nurture, and celebrate trees. Visitors can explore the interactive Tree Adventure, tour the historic 52-room Arbor Lodge Mansion, and enjoy seasonal activities like apple picking and discovery rides. It serves as a nature-focused resort and educational destination that combines conservation history with outdoor fun for all ages. Arbor Day Farm About
attractions.hours: Tree Adventure: Tue-Fri 9:00 am - 5:00 pm, Sat 10:00 am - 5:00 pm, Sun 11:00 am - 5:00 pm. Apple House Market: Tue-Sat 10:00 am - 5:00 pm, Sun 11:00 am - 5:00 pm. Arbor Lodge Mansion: Tue-Sun 11:00 am - 5:00 pm. (Closed Mondays) Arbor Day Farm Contact
attractions.admission: Tree Adventure (Adult 13+): $16.55, Tree Adventure (Child 3-12): $13.44, Tree Adventure (Under 3): Free. Arbor Lodge Mansion (Adult): $9.00, Arbor Lodge Mansion (Child): $7.00. Combo Tickets (Tree Adventure + Arbor Lodge): Adult $21.72, Child $17.58. Discovery Ride Add-On: $8.27. (Note: prices include sales tax and fees) Arbor Day Farm Tickets
highlights: Treetop Village: A unique 3-acre experience featuring 11 treehouses connected by netted bridges and the giant WonderNet.
Arbor Lodge State Historical Park: Home to the 52-room mansion of Arbor Day founder J. Sterling Morton and beautiful Italian terraced gardens.
Apple House Market: A year-round market where visitors can shop for local goods, sip apple cider slushies, and taste wines.
Discovery Ride: A one-hour guided tractor-ride tour through the farm's backwoods, orchards, and vineyards (seasonal).
Nature Trails: Miles of scenic trails, including the South Table Creek Trail and the paved Doug's Trail connecting Lied Lodge to the Tree Adventure.
Lied Lodge: A world-class timber-frame resort and conference center focused on conservation and hospitality.
attractions.tips: Wear athletic or closed-toe shoes for the Tree Adventure and Treetop Village to avoid getting feet stuck in the netting. Stroller parking is available at Treetop Village, but the bridges themselves are not handicap accessible (though there is an accessible boardwalk nearby). Book tickets online in advance for events like Discovery Rides or Sunset & S'mores, as they can sell out. Loving This Adventure

Originally opened in 1976 and extensively renovated in 2013, the facility features a world-class volleyball arena (John Cook Arena), a hydraulic-banked indoor track, and a 1,000-seat natatorium. attractions.name: Bodega's Alley
attractions.description: Bodega's Alley is a legendary downtown Lincoln staple recognized as the city's premier destination for the jam band music scene since its establishment in 1996. After a brief closure in late 2023, the venue reopened to continue its legacy of hosting live original music, ranging from local acts to nationally touring bands, in a vibrant atmosphere with exceptional sound and lighting. The bar offers a full drink menu, including a popular happy hour, and serves as a key anchor in the historic O Street entertainment district. (Downtown Lincoln Association, Hear Nebraska)

Regular participant in major local music events like the Lincoln Exposed festival.

Known for a welcoming, high-energy atmosphere with a full-service bar.
Centrally located in the heart of the Downtown Lincoln entertainment district.
attractions.tips: The venue is strictly 21+ for all shows, and visitors should check their social media for the most current live music schedule and any specific cover charges for the night. (ArtistAndFan, Instagram)

attractions.description: Branched Oak State Recreation Area features the largest lake in eastern Nebraska and is a premier destination for water sports and outdoor activities. The 1,800-acre lake is surrounded by over 5,000 acres of scenic grounds, offering extensive camping, fishing, and boating opportunities. Nebraska Game & Parks Commission

attractions.name: Capitol Beach Lake (Salt Lake)
attractions.description: Capitol Beach Lake is a 300-acre private saltwater lake located just three miles west of downtown Lincoln, Nebraska. Originally a natural saline wetland known as the "Great Salt Lake" of the plains, it was developed into a resort in the late 1800s and later a residential neighborhood that offers unique views of the Nebraska State Capitol. Today, the area serves as a private recreational hub for boating and fishing while its adjacent public nature center protects rare saline wetland ecosystems. (CBCA, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 570-598 W Industrial Lake Dr (Saline Wetlands Nature Center access)
attractions.hours: Daily: Sunrise to Sunset (Personal watercraft prohibited between sunset and sunrise) (CBCA, Wikipedia)
attractions.admission: Private (Members only for lake access); Free (Public access to Saline Wetlands Nature Center) (Homes.com, Wikipedia)
highlights: Unique saltwater lake ecosystem in the heart of Nebraska
Panoramic views of the Nebraska State Capitol and downtown Lincoln skyline
Home to diverse wildlife including blue herons, minks, and foxes
Recreational water sports such as sailing, water-skiing, and jet skiing for members
Historic significance as a major salt production site in the 19th century
Public nature trails available at the adjacent Lincoln Saline Wetlands Nature Center
attractions.tips: Note that the lake itself is private and restricted to residents and members; public visitors should use the Lincoln Saline Wetlands Nature Center on the east shore for public trails and views. Bring a camera for scenic sunsets and binoculars for birdwatching in the rare saline wetlands. (Homes.com, Reddit)
attractions.nearby: Nebraska State Capitol, Downtown Lincoln, Sun Valley Boulevard, Oak Creek (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Chimney Rock National Historic Site
attractions.description: Chimney Rock is a famous natural landmark that served as a critical guidepost for hundreds of thousands of pioneers traveling west on the Oregon, California, and Mormon Trails. Rising nearly 300 feet above the North Platte River valley, its unique spire signaled the end of the plains and the beginning of the difficult mountain passage. Today, the site includes a visitor center with interpretive exhibits, interactive displays, and a film detailing the emigrant experience. National Park Service, History Nebraska
attractions.hours: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM MT daily during the peak season (May 1 – September 30); 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM MT Tuesday – Saturday during the off-season (October 1 – April 30). History Nebraska
attractions.days_open: May 1 – September 30: Daily; October 1 – April 30: Tuesday – Saturday. History Nebraska
attractions.admission: Adults (19+): $8.00; Children (5-18): $4.00; Children (4 & Under): Free; History Nebraska Members: Free. Reduced rates ($2 adult/$1 child) available for SNAP EBT cardholders via Museums for All. History Nebraska, Visit Nebraska
highlights: Towering natural spire reaching 4,226 feet above sea level
Ethel S. and Christopher J. Abbott Visitor Center featuring museum exhibits
Interactive 'pioneer loading' displays and interpretive videos about the westward migration
Designated National Historic Site since 1956 and an Oregon Trail landmark
Walkable trails near the base of the rock for close-up viewing and photography
attractions.tips: Stay on marked trails to avoid rattlesnakes and prickly plants; sunset is the ideal time for photography as the rock glows against the horizon; for a free closer view, drive past the visitor center to the dead-end gravel road trailhead. Yelp, Wikivoyage

It is famous for serving the original Blackstone Reuben sandwich and features over 65 beers on tap across its main level and the downstairs Huber Haus German Bier Hall. Crescent Moon Alehouse

attractions.name: Don L. Love Memorial Library
attractions.description: Don L. Love Memorial Library is the flagship research facility for the University of Nebraska–Lincoln, housing a collection of over three million volumes. The complex consists of the historic Love South and modern Love North buildings, which are connected by a central "Link" and contain the Adele Hall Learning Commons. It serves as a vital academic hub offering specialized study spaces, archives, and extensive digital and physical resources for the university community and researchers. UNL Libraries, UNL MediaHub
attractions.address: 318 Love Library (13th & R Streets), University of Nebraska-Lincoln, Lincoln, NE 68588-4100
attractions.hours: Mon: 7:30 am - 5:00 pm, Tue: 7:30 am - 5:00 pm, Wed: 7:30 am - 5:00 pm, Thu: 7:30 am - 5:00 pm, Fri: 7:30 am - 5:00 pm, Sat: Closed, Sun: Closed (Hours reflect summer/transition schedule for May 2026). UNL Libraries Hours

attractions.name: Historic Haymarket District (VisitNebraska.com)
attractions.description: The Historic Haymarket District is a vibrant revitalized warehouse district in downtown Lincoln that is listed on the National Register of Historic Places. It features restored turn-of-the-century architecture that now houses unique boutiques, art galleries, diverse restaurants, and bustling breweries. The area serves as a premier cultural hub, hosting popular events like the Haymarket Farmers' Market and offering a lively nightlife scene near Pinnacle Bank Arena. (VisitNebraska.com, Wikipedia))
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: District is a public area and always open; individual business hours typically vary from 10:00 AM to 8:30 PM (Kupi.com)
attractions.admission: Free (Airial Travel)
highlights: Restored turn-of-the-century warehouses with unique brick architecture (VisitNebraska.com)
Haymarket Farmers' Market held every Saturday morning from May to October (Downtown Lincoln Association)
Pinnacle Bank Arena and Haymarket Park baseball stadium for sports and entertainment (Wikipedia))
Iron Horse Park featuring historic murals, sculptures, and a restored water tower (Downtown Lincoln Association)

attractions.tips: The first hour of parking is free in the Haymarket Garage; the best time for photography is during the golden hour to capture the historic brick facades. Visit on Saturday mornings for the full buzz of the Farmers' Market. (Airial Travel, Kupi.com)

attractions.name: Homestead National Historical Park
attractions.description: Homestead National Historical Park commemorates the Homestead Act of 1862, which granted 160 acres of free land to settlers in exchange for five years of residence and cultivation. The park is situated on the site of one of the first successful homestead claims and features a variety of exhibits on westward expansion and pioneer life.
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday-Sunday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Summer hours: 8:30 AM - 6:00 PM daily)
attractions.days_open: Every Day (Closed New Year's Day, Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Heritage Center and Museum with interactive exhibits on the Homestead Act's global impact
Restored Tallgrass Prairie featuring nearly 3 miles of scenic hiking trails
Palmer-Epard Cabin, an original 1867 log home moved to the site for preservation
Education Center featuring the Farm Implement Room with historic agricultural tools
Freeman School, a historic one-room brick schoolhouse dating back to 1872
Research computers for visitors to search their own ancestry and homestead records
attractions.tips: Allow at least 2.5 hours to fully explore the museum, watch the introductory film, and walk the trails. Don't miss the 23-minute 'Land of Dreams' film at the Heritage Center for essential context. The site can be very hot in summer, so bring water and wear sun protection when hiking the prairie trails.

Its LEED-certified building, designed by Robert A. M. Stern Architects, features multiple galleries with frequently rotating exhibitions. Official Site, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Lincoln Army Air Field Regimental Chapel (Air Park Historic Site)
attractions.description: The Lincoln Army Air Field Regimental Chapel is a remarkably well-preserved World War II-era military chapel and the only mobilization chapel in Nebraska that remains on its original site. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places, it represents the significant military history of the Air Park neighborhood, which served as a major aviation training and mechanics hub during World War II and the Cold War.
attractions.hours: Sunday: 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM, 6:30 PM - 8:30 PM; Monday: 5:30 AM, 3:00 PM; Tuesday: 3:00 PM, 7:00 PM; Wednesday: 3:00 PM, 6:00 PM - 8:30 PM; Thursday: 3:00 PM, 6:30 PM; Friday: 3:00 PM; Saturday: Closed (Service and prayer line hours; other visits by appointment)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Listed on the National Register of Historic Places (NRHP) since 1993.
The only World War II mobilization chapel in Nebraska still on its original site.
Constructed in May 1942 in only 21 days as part of the Lincoln Army Air Field.
Features intact Colonial Revival architecture and original interior furnishings, including pine pews and the chancel rail.
Significantly associated with the training of over 65,000 military personnel during the war.
Now owned by the Lincoln Housing Authority and serves as a community landmark for the Air Park area.
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to respect the active congregation's service schedule when visiting the interior. The chapel is available for rental or public use through the Lincoln Housing Authority, and more general neighborhood amenities can be found at the adjacent Air Park Community Center.

attractions.name: Thomas P. Kennard House (Nebraska Statehood Memorial)
attractions.description: The Thomas P. Kennard House, also known as the Nebraska Statehood Memorial, is the oldest remaining building in the original plat of Lincoln. Built in 1869 in the elaborate Italianate style, it served as the residence of Nebraska's first Secretary of State and was intended to instill confidence in the newly established capital city. (Wikipedia, History Nebraska)
attractions.region: Original plat of Lincoln (Downtown area) (Wikipedia, History Nebraska)
attractions.hours: By appointment only (History Nebraska, Visit Nebraska)
attractions.days_open: Open by appointment only (History Nebraska, Visit Nebraska)
attractions.admission: Free (Donations appreciated) (History Nebraska, Visit Nebraska)
highlights: Oldest residence in Lincoln's original 1867 plat (Wikipedia)
Designated as the Nebraska Statehood Memorial in 1965 (History Nebraska)
Exquisite Italianate architecture designed by Chicago architect John Keys Winchell (Wikipedia)
Restored interior reflecting 1870s Victorian lifestyle and formality (Visit Nebraska)
Backyard features marked archaeological excavation sites for self-guided exploration (Nebraska Archaeology)
Historical connection to the 'Father of Lincoln,' Thomas P. Kennard (E Nebraska History)
attractions.tips: Visitors must call ahead or contact History Nebraska to arrange a tour, as the house is open by appointment only. Special festive tours are sometimes held during the holiday season in December. (Visit Nebraska, Airial Travel)
attractions.nearby: Nebraska State Capitol, Ferguson House, Hurlbut-Yates House, Nebraska Governor's Mansion, Lincoln Children's Museum, Lincoln Children's Zoo (Lincoln Today Magazine, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: University of Nebraska State Museum (Morrill Hall)
attractions.description: Established in 1871, the University of Nebraska State Museum is the state's premier natural history institution, famous for its world-class collection of fossil elephants. Housed in Morrill Hall on the UNL campus, it features four floors of interactive exhibits spanning paleontology, wildlife, and world cultures. Morrill Hall Home
attractions.hours: Tuesday - Saturday: 9:00am - 5:00pm, Sunday: 12:00pm - 5:00pm, Monday: Closed. Morrill Hall Visit
attractions.admission: Adults (ages 12+): $13.00, Children (ages 3-11): $7.50, Young Children (under 3): FREE, Museum Members/UNL NCard Holders/ASTC Members: FREE. A $1 discount is available for Seniors (65+) and Military. Morrill Hall Visit
highlights: Elephant Hall featuring 'Archie,' the world's largest fully mounted Columbian mammoth fossil.
Mueller Planetarium, an immersive 360-degree full-dome digital theater.
Cherish Nebraska, a floor dedicated to the state's natural heritage and biodiversity.
Visible Lab where visitors can watch researchers prepare actual paleontological specimens.
Marx Discovery Center offering hands-on science activities for children.
Mesozoic Gallery showcasing ancient marine reptiles and dinosaur fossils from Nebraska.
attractions.tips: Free museum parking is limited to the east of the building; avoid UNL staff/student spots to prevent tickets. Plan for 2-3 hours to see all four floors, or a half-day if attending a planetarium show. Morrill Hall Visit

attractions.name: Wagon Train State Recreation Area
attractions.description: Wagon Train State Recreation Area is a 1,061-acre park featuring a 315-acre lake surrounded by 746 acres of land located just east of Hickman and within 25 miles of Lincoln. The area is a popular destination for water-oriented activities, offering a quiet environment for fishing, boating, and both electric and primitive camping (Visit Nebraska, City of Hickman).
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily year-round for camping; Day-use areas generally follow sunrise to sunset (Nebraska Game & Parks Commission)
attractions.admission: Nebraska resident: $7 daily / $35 annual; Non-resident: $14 daily / $70 annual (Nebraska Game & Parks Commission)
highlights: 315-acre lake for no-wake boating and fishing (Outdoor Nebraska)
28 camping pads with 20, 30, and 50-amp electrical hookups (Visit Nebraska)
Unsupervised swimming beach on the east shore (Outdoor Nebraska)
Diverse fish species including Largemouth Bass, Walleye, and Muskellunge (Outdoor Nebraska)
Picnic areas with shelters and a playground for families (Corps Lakes Gateway)
Dog training area and 746 acres of land for hiking and wildlife viewing (Corps Lakes Gateway)
attractions.tips: A park entry permit is required for all vehicles and can be purchased online or at the park. Camping is first-come, first-served, and alcoholic beverages are prohibited from 10 p.m. to 6 a.m. in all areas (Outdoor Nebraska, Outdoor Nebraska FAQ).
attractions.nearby: Stagecoach State Recreation Area (Outdoor Nebraska), Bluestem State Recreation Area (Outdoor Nebraska), City of Lincoln (Visit Nebraska)

attractions.name: Wilderness Park (City of Lincoln)
attractions.description: Wilderness Park is Lincoln's largest park, spanning 1,472 acres of natural area that straddles the Salt Creek floodplain. It features over 30 miles of multi-use trails through dense forest, meadows, and wetlands, offering a unique wilderness experience within an urban setting (City of Lincoln).
attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset (City of Lincoln Master Plan)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: Lincoln's largest park at 1,472 acres (City of Lincoln)
Over 30 miles of multi-use trails for hiking, biking, and horseback riding (City of Lincoln)
Diverse ecosystems including tallgrass prairie, wetlands, and dense woodlands (City of Lincoln)
Historic features such as the Epworth Park Entry Arch (City of Lincoln)
Connection to the Jamaica North Trail and regional trail systems (City of Lincoln)
Abundant wildlife spotting opportunities for deer, foxes, and numerous bird species (City of Lincoln)
attractions.tips: Avoid using trails when they are muddy to prevent damage; always yield to horses; check for ticks after your visit (City of Lincoln, Reddit).
attractions.nearby: Pioneers Park Nature Center, WunderRosa Winery, Roca Berry Farm, Densmore Park, Sunken Gardens (Tripadvisor, City of Lincoln)

attractions.name: Wyuka Cemetery and Park (also known as Wyuka Cemetery and Funeral Home)
attractions.description: Wyuka Cemetery and Funeral Home is a nationally recognized historic cemetery and the largest all-faith cemetery serving the Lincoln area. Established in 1869, it encompasses over 140 acres of rolling terrain designed in the "rural cemetery" style with scenic curving roads and diverse mature trees (Wyuka About).
attractions.region: East Lincoln (Bounded by O, Vine, North 36th, and North 42nd Streets) (Preservation Association of Lincoln)
attractions.hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Saturday 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM; Gates: Daily 8:00 AM to Dusk (Wyuka Cemetery Page)
attractions.admission: Free for general grounds access; $5 for the "Wyuka Cemetery: A Driving & Walking Tour" guide book (Wyuka Cemetery Page)
highlights: Listed on the National Register of Historic Places (1982) (Wikipedia)
Home to the Nebraska Holocaust Memorial, dedicated in 2007 (Wikipedia)
Includes the 9/11 Memorial, Nebraska Firefighters Memorial, and Purple Heart Monument (Adventures in Cemetery Hopping)
Features the historic Soldiers' Circle and GAR Section for Civil War veterans (Adventures in Cemetery Hopping)
Contains the Gothic Revival style Rudge Memorial Chapel, completed in 1938 (Preservation Association of Lincoln)
The surrounding iron fence was originally erected around the University of Nebraska-Lincoln campus in 1891 (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Stop at the main office to purchase the $5 driving and walking tour book. Be mindful that it is an active cemetery and respect grieving families who may be present. Use the online grave locator on the official website to find specific interment sites (Wyuka Cemetery Page).

attractions.name: Zipline Brewing Co.
attractions.description: Founded in 2012, Zipline Brewing Co. is a prominent craft brewery in Lincoln dedicated to creating artisan ales and lagers for life’s next adventures. Their production facility and taproom offer a wide range of award-winning beers, from crisp German-style lagers to bold IPAs, all served in an industrial-chic atmosphere. Zipline Brewing, Lincoln by Mouth
